title: Execution Report Contract
description: Deterministic execution report capturing the outcome of executing a validated plan. This report is authoritative, auditable, and suitable for both human review and machine analysis.

execution_id.description: Unique identifier for this execution run. Used to correlate reports, logs, and downstream artifacts.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp indicating when the execution report was generated.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp indicating when execution started.

finished_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp indicating when execution finished.

dry_run.description: Indicates whether this execution was performed in dry-run mode, meaning no filesystem mutation occurred.

results.description: Ordered list of per-action execution results, one entry for each action evaluated by the executor.

action_id.description: Identifier of the action from the execution plan to which this result corresponds.

handler.description: Name of the execution handler or executor responsible for processing this action.

dry_run.description: Indicates whether this specific action was evaluated in dry-run mode.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of what occurred during execution of the action.

error.description: Raw error message if the action failed. Null if the action did not encounter an error.

error_type.description: Structured classification of the failure. Null if the action did not fail.

metadata.description: Optional structured metadata emitted by the handler for diagnostic or downstream processing purposes.

summary.description: Aggregate counts summarizing the execution outcomes across all actions.

success.description: Number of actions that completed successfully.

failed.description: Number of actions that failed during execution.

skipped.description: Number of actions that were intentionally skipped, including dry-run skips and idempotent no-ops.

total.description: Total number of actions evaluated during execution.

discovery.description: Discovery-level context surfaced in the execution report to improve troubleshooting.

missing_references.description: Count of unresolved internal references discovered upstream.

external_references.description: Count of references pointing outside the package.
